constraints.expression: consumer verifies Ed25519 signature_value over sha256:safeCanonicalize(payload-minus-signatures-minus-signed_payload_hash) using the public_key referenced by SignerEntry.key_ref; consumer recomputes signed_payload_hash via safeCanonicalize and rejects on mismatch
constraints.message: SignatureEnvelope cryptographic verification is consumer-side per ADR-010 / G1; library does NOT verify and emits CRYPTO_DEFERRED on default validate() calls.
constraints.evaluation_note: Crypto-bearing per the v8.5.0 boundary (ADR-010). validate(SignatureEnvelopeSchema, payload) defaults to { valid: false, errors: [{ code: 'CRYPTO_DEFERRED' }] }; consumer opt-in via { acceptDeferred: true } returns { valid: true, unverified_obligations: [...] } with the obligation manifest entry. Consumer obligations: (1) recompute the signed_payload_hash via safeCanonicalize over the wire artifact's other fields (excluding signatures[] and the hash field itself); (2) reject if the recomputed hash differs from the carried signed_payload_hash; (3) verify the Ed25519 signature_value against the public key referenced by the matching SignerEntry; (4) confirm the signer's CapabilityScopedTrust profile satisfies the action's SignerCompetenceRule.required_capability_scopes per the configured match_strategy.
